author: sukunrt
content:
<@412614104222531604> I don't know the state of development but this is how I understand it. 

- getBlobsV3: takes a list of versioned hashes and returns the entire blob, not just the cells we're interested in.

  In partial messages, fetching existing blobs from EL is in the forwarding path for gossipsub. In metrics we see this call taking 100-500ms time. So we have two options to improve this. 

- getBlobsV4: This is a proposed API for sparse blobpool change. This API returns only the cells we need and not the entire blobs. Compared to V3 this will improve performance because there's less data to send across to the CL. 

- hasBlobs: This should fix this completely. It will return whether or not the blobs/cells are present in the EL. It won't return the data itself which is fine for us because all we care about is which blobs are available and which aren't for the purpose of sending our partsMetadata to our partial message peers. We can fetch the data in parallel with the getBlobsV3 or getBlobsV4 call.   

I think we're going for hasBlobs because it'll help supernodes and ELs can cache presence / absence of blobs in memory and not need a disk access for this API.

author: barnabasbusa
content:
can we do v4 without v3? 
What's the implementaiton dependnecy graph look like?

author: danielknopik
content:
I am not an EL dev, but i see no reason why a client should not be able to support v4 only. 

but I believe all clients already support v3

author: barnabasbusa
content:
All ELs support v3
geth supports v4

I'm asking about the CL depenedency mainly, as only 2 clients have done v3 and no upstream project has done v4 yet

author: barnabasbusa
content: And the question is whether CLs could just to v4 without v3

author: danielknopik
content:
ah, makes sense

AFAIK, there is no use case that is covered by v3 but not by v4. Or in other words, once LH is extended to support v4, it will always use it if the connected EL supports it

author: danielknopik
content: and of course other CL clients can just implement v4 as soon as it is widely supported on EL

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: Yeah and there is absolutely no need to add support for v4 right now atleast for the partial cells workstream

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: (HasBlobs + GetBlobsV3)  should be enough. IIUC, we didn't find any conclusive evidence that v4 is faster than v3.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
That is a lot more work and I'd like to do it outside the partial cells workstream as a seperate workstream of it's own. As I said, without the sparse blobpool, there is no meaningful perf diff b/w v3 and v4.

What HasBlobs gets us is a quick way to answer "what blobs do I have in the EL mempool" without doing a disk read (GetBlobsV3/V4 always hit the disk). Then we can quickly advertise our missing cells so nodes can start sending us those. IMO, it's orthognal to V3/V4.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: We can do (HasBlobs + GetBlobsV4) or (HasBlobs + GetBlobsV4)
